Text: What does 'git rebase' do differently from 'git merge'?
Options: Rebase is slower than merge;Rebase replays commits from one branch onto another producing a linear history while merge creates a merge commit preserving branch history

Text: What is the purpose of a .gitignore file?
Options: To ignore syntax errors;To tell Git which files or directories to exclude from version control
Permanently deletes uncommitted changes;Temporarily saves uncommitted changes so you can switch context and apply them later

Text: What is a pull request (PR) / merge request (MR)?
Options: A Git command to pull code;A collaborative review mechanism where a developer proposes changes for review before merging into a target branch

Text: What is the Git 'cherry-pick' command used for?
Options: Selecting files for staging;Applying a specific commit from one branch to another without merging the entire branch
Undoes the last commit and stages changes;Undoes the last commit and discards all changes permanently moving HEAD back by one commit

Text: What is the SOLID principle 'Single Responsibility Principle'?
Options: A class must have exactly one method;A class should have only one reason to change meaning it should encapsulate only one responsibility or concern

Text: What is the DRY principle in software engineering?
Options: Use dynamic types everywhere;Do Not Repeat Yourself — avoid duplicating logic or knowledge by extracting it into a single reusable abstraction
A syntax error;An indicator in source code that suggests a deeper problem — not a bug itself but a sign the design could be improved
Writing tests after shipping code;A development approach where tests are written before implementation driving design and ensuring coverage

Text: What is the purpose of semantic versioning (e.g. 2.4.1)?
Options: To number releases randomly;Major.Minor.Patch — Major breaks compatibility Minor adds features backward-compatibly Patch fixes bugs

Text: What is the purpose of code review?
Options: To increase code size;To catch bugs improve code quality share knowledge and ensure adherence to standards before merging changes

Text: Write a JavaScript function called 'debounce' that takes a function fn and a delay in milliseconds and returns a debounced version that only calls fn after the delay has elapsed since the last invocation.
Write a JavaScript function called 'memoize' that takes any function and returns a memoized version that caches results by serialising arguments as the cache key.

Write a JavaScript function called 'pipe' that takes any number of functions and returns a new function that applies them left to right passing the output of each as the input to the next. Example: pipe(double add1)(5) = 11.
